project: Forge: AI-powered project management with issue tracking, agent execution, and multi-client support
architecture: Web/Desktop UIs → core (Hono) REST API + WebSocket → Postgres (Drizzle). core (packages/core) is a single Node process that serves REST, WebSocket, and an MCP server, and runs AI agents via Claude CLI or cloud APIs.

conventions.naming: kebab-case dirs, camelCase TS, snake_case Rust. Feature-based organization in web/dev.
conventions.api: Hono routes mounted under /api/<plural> in packages/core. Bearer token auth. WebSocket at /ws for real-time.
conventions.state: React Query for server state (web/dev). Zustand for client state (dev). Drizzle + DB triggers / route hooks for cascading updates in core.

branchStrategy.summary: Two-layer branch resolution. Each issue's effective {baseBranch, targetBranch, prodBranch} is computed field-by-field: issue override → project default → literal 'main'. See CLAUDE.md § Branching strategy for the prose version.
branchStrategy.targetBranchFallback: targetBranch has no dedicated project column; if neither layer sets it, it falls through to the resolved baseBranch.
branchStrategy.resolver: packages/core/src/branches/resolve.ts → resolveIssueBranches(issue, project) returns { baseBranch, targetBranch, prodBranch }.
branchStrategy.webMirror: packages/web/src/features/issue/hooks/use-resolved-branch-config.ts (same logic, runs against React Query state).
branchStrategy.skillsContract: Pipeline skills (forge-plan, forge-code, forge-release, forge-fix) MUST resolve via forge_config get { issueId } and use $BASE — never hardcode 'main'.

projectDefault.effect: Changing the project default affects FUTURE issues. In-flight issues keep their resolved value until they get a per-issue override.

issueOverride.ui: Issue detail page → aside → 'Branch config' card → toggle 'Override project branch config'.
issueOverride.storage: issues.metadata.branchConfig (jsonb): { baseBranch?: string|null, targetBranch?: string|null, prodBranch?: string|null }
validation: Cannot equal the issue's own iss-<seq> (would create a fetch loop).
Empty strings are rejected — pass null to clear an override.
issueOverride.wireFormat: PATCH /api/projects/:projectId/issues/:id with body { metadata: { branchConfig: { baseBranch, targetBranch, prodBranch } } }.

branchStrategy.schemaMigration: packages/core/drizzle/migrations/0061_issue_metadata.sql adds issues.metadata jsonb; validator at packages/core/src/issues/metadata.ts.
branchStrategy.whenToOverride: Decomposed-epic integration branches (auto-set — see recipes.decomposition-integration-branch). Hot-fix bases off an older release tag. Working a branch off another in-flight branch. Otherwise leave blank.

recipes.new-endpoint: 1) Create packages/core/src/{name}/routes.ts (+ tests). 2) Mount in packages/core/src/index.ts. 3) Add WS broadcast if needed.
recipes.new-page-web: 1) Create packages/web/src/app/(protected)/{path}/page.tsx. 2) Add feature module in packages/web/src/features/{name}/ with api.ts, types.ts, hooks/.
recipes.new-agent-tool: 1) Add tool in packages/core/src/mcp/tools/forge-{name}.ts. 2) Register in packages/core/src/mcp/server.ts.

decomposition-integration-branch.when: Complex epic with ≥3 sub-issues that share UI/data contracts AND live in a single package AND should ship as one atomic release. Use the shared integration branch so sub-issues merge into it (not the project trunk) and the parent pipeline merges integration → production once all children land. SKIP for cross-package work, core refactors, single-sub decompositions, or quick-fix bundles — use flat-to-trunk merges instead. Full when/when-not list: CLAUDE.md § Branching strategy → 'When to use decomposition + integration branch'.
steps: Decide opt-in. Default useIntegrationBranch=true. To opt out (children branch off the project trunk individually), pass decomposeOpts: { useIntegrationBranch: false } on the FIRST forge_pm_set_dependency call against the parent.
For each child workstream, create the child issue at status='on_hold' (see SKILL.md Step 5.5) so cascade-approve takes over after the parent is approved.

Call forge_pm_set_dependency { projectId, fromIssueId: <parent>, toIssueId: <child>, kind: 'decomposes' } once per child. The FIRST call auto-creates the integration branch iss-<parentSeq>-<slug> off the project's baseBranch, git-pushes it inside the same DB transaction, and writes branchConfig.baseBranch=<integrationBranch> to both parent.metadata and child.metadata.
Verify the dependency edge landed: every call returns { id, created: true|false }. If FORBIDDEN / CYCLE_DETECTED is thrown, stop and post a comment — silent failure here is the ISS-131 footgun.

If the parent plan declares sibling-blocks ordering (Sub2 must wait for Sub1), add forge_pm_set_dependency { kind: 'blocks' } edges immediately after creating all children. L2 dispatcher evaluates blocks at dispatch time for every job type.
Set the parent to status='waiting' (Complex requires human approval). Cascade-approve flips children on_hold → approved when the parent is approved.
Each child's forge-code resolves its base via forge_config and checks out iss-<parentSeq>-<slug>. PRs merge back onto the integration branch, not main.

When the LAST child reaches staging, the watcher re-fires the parent pipeline. forge-test runs integration checks against the merged integration branch; forge-release merges integration → production atomically (L2 release gate 'waiting_on_decomp_parent' clears for every child).
Manual cleanup: delete the remote integration branch after release lands (auto-delete is a post-ISS-138 follow-up).
gotchas: Opt-out is per-decomposition, not per-child. useIntegrationBranch is read from the FIRST forge_pm_set_dependency call only; subsequent calls inherit parent.metadata.useIntegrationBranch.
Integration-branch creation runs inside the dependency-edge DB transaction. If git push fails, the row rolls back and the child appears un-decomposed — re-run the call after fixing the push.
Branch-name conflicts auto-suffix -2..-10. Past 10 slots, decompose() throws and the parent stays un-decomposed — slugify the title differently or close stale integration branches first.
Auto-create only fires when parent.status ∈ {confirmed, waiting}. Subsequent forge_pm_set_dependency calls on the same parent are allowed in any status but won't re-create the branch.

Cross-package epics (core + web + dev) accumulate merge drift on a long-lived integration branch faster than the integration win pays back — prefer flat-to-trunk merges with feature flags hiding incomplete work.
Core refactors (auth, state machine, runner protocol) must NEVER use an integration branch — every diff against main must be independently reviewable for security/correctness.
relatedRefs: CLAUDE.md § Branching strategy → Decomposition + integration branch
